quotes.text: Technically speaking, the building is still standing.
quotes.speaker: Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb (1964)
quotes.context: A Pentagon aide, deadpan, trying to soften catastrophically bad news for the generals
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Kubrick's film is wall-to-wall bureaucratic understatement played for dark comedy, and this kind of hair-splitting damage control fits the War Room scenes precisely. The giveaway is it's almost too clean a punchline; the real film's humor tends to come from longer, more convoluted official-speak rather than a single crisp sentence.

quotes.text: You play the same four notes every night and somehow it still sounds like the first time.
